name: Chocolate Liqueur Shells
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Each of semi-sweet or Bittersweet, milk and white chocolate, melted in separate bowls
1 tbsp Each of Tia Maria, Creme de Menthe or:
recipeInstructions:
1. With a spoon, smear melted chocolate evenly over the inside of 12 paper cups.
2. Turn cups upside down on a plate and refrigerate until set. Gently peel off the paper.
3. Mousse: Slowly melt white chocolate. Remove from heat; quickly beat in egg yolks. Set aside.
4. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry. Divide yolk mix into three bowls and add 1 teaspoon of a different liqueur to each bowl.
5. Add a drop or two of green food coloring to the bowl containing creme de menthe if desired. A drop or two of yellow coloring can be added to Cointreau mixture.
6. Gently fold a third of the egg whites into each bowl.
7. Spoon mousse into chocolate shells and refrigerate for 2 hours.
8. These shells should be consumed within 24 hours. The chocolate cases can be made ahead of time and stored in a cool, dry place. Makes 12 candies.
